setting.note: Emma's world is the invented village of Yonville-l'Abbaye, drawn from the real Ry east of Rouen, and Tostes (now Tôtes) to the north, where Charles first practises. Her escapes are to a real Rouen: the cathedral, and the closed cab that scandalised France, circling the city with its blinds down. That drive can be walked.

rights.originalWork: Public domain. Gustave Flaubert, first published 1856; the author died in 1880.
rights.translation: English translation by Eleanor Marx-Aveling (1886); she died in 1898, so the translation is public domain in the US and UK/EU.
rights.territoryNotes: Public domain in the US and UK/EU: Flaubert died 1880, Marx-Aveling 1898.
rights.attribution: Gustave Flaubert, Madame Bovary (1856), trans. Eleanor Marx-Aveling. Text from Project Gutenberg.

palette.accentSourceQuote: A great red glow passed over her
palette.note: The red firelight that plays over Emma at the Lion d'Or the evening she first meets Léon. A deep red that clears WCAG AA on paper.

charles.name: Charles Bovary
charles.colorSourceQuote: his short school jacket of green cloth
